theme: analog circuit meets spec at every tolerance corner
title: Matched-Batch Ladder: Dividers That Hold at Every Tolerance Corner
strategies: trivial: realize every tap as a 1/2 divider from two matched units of value[0] (corner-invariant but ignores the targets) -> reproduces the checker baseline

greedy nominal fit: one distinct catalog value on top, one on bottom (count 1 each) closest to each target ratio -- nails nominal, but two different values carry independent corner signs and blow up at the worst corner

strong insight: since same-valued parts share one corner sign, build each tap from a matched integer ratio b/(a+b) of a single value so the correlated error cancels EXACTLY at every corner; residual is only the rational-approximation error
ceiling above strong: push the matched-ratio resolution (larger a+b) and spend the part budget on the hardest taps to shrink the residual further
